channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Wearables Are Getting Very Messy
item.description: Whoop, there it is. And by "it" we mean another lawsuit.

item.title: Cette trottinette électrique Navee à -43% concurrence sérieusement Xiaomi et Segway, avec supplément RGB pour le style
item.description: La Navee ST3 Pro est un modèle de trottinette électrique destiné aux trajets urbains quotidiens qui mise sur le confort et la fiabilité, à retrouver pour 449,98 euros chez Electro Depot ou 470,23 euros sur Amazon, au lieu de 799 euros.

item.title: Aiper IrriSense 2 Smart Irrigation System Review: Clever Yet Uneven
item.description: This hose-attached smart sprinkler maps your yard, rotates on command, and measures water use. Despite these clever features, the results are a little uneven.

item.title: I've driven the new Renault Twingo — and it's the closest thing to affordable, lightweight electric motoring without the compromise
item.description: Renault continues its run of excellent retro reimagining with the cute, compact Twingo. Affordable electric motoring done right.

item.title: Super Meat Boy 3D, coin-pushing chaos and other new indie games worth checking out
item.description: Welcome to our latest roundup of what's going on in the indie game space. As ever, we've got a whole bunch of new games for you to dive into this weekend, along with announcements and updates on several others that are coming down the pike.I love how spoiled we are for game showcases these days, and I'm really looking forward to the latest installment of the indie-focused Triple-i Initiative at noon ET on April 9 as the first two editions were really strong. The trailer for this one features the likes of Cairn, Warhammer Survivors (the Warhammer-themed Vampire Survivors spinoff), the excellent...

item.title: Hackers Are Posting the Claude Code Leak With Bonus Malware
item.description: Plus: The FBI says a recent hack of its wiretap tools poses a national security risk, attackers stole Cisco source code as part of an ongoing supply chain hacking spree, and more.

item.title: Pourquoi la Nintendo Switch 2 cache en fait un port SSD qui ne dit pas son nom
item.description: On savait que la Switch 2 passait au microSD Express, mais on n'avait pas encore mesuré l'ampleur du potentiel. Le moddeur Mizuyoukan a réussi un bel exploit : brancher un SSD NVMe standard directement sur le port de la console.

item.title: Trump Wants to Start Privatizing TSA in Latest Budget Proposal
item.description: "The Budget begins the privatization of TSA’s airport screeners...."

item.title: Lost Warship From Battle of Copenhagen Found After 225 Years
item.description: Within the shipwreck lay cannons, shoes, personal belongings, and bones of soldiers who went down with their ship.

item.title: These One-of-a-Kind Objects Are in the Wrong Museums
How to Get Reliable Wi-Fi in Your Backyard
item.description: There are several ways to extend your Wi-Fi network into your backyard and out onto the deck or patio. Here’s how.

item.title: Sivga's winsome wired headphones look the part but, in testing, I found the bass was a bulging mess of directionless energy
item.description: The Sivga Robin SV021 Pro are wired over-ear headphones that punch above their budget, just about, but the bass is a mess.

item.title: Spotify’s SongDNA is like a musical Wikipedia on steroids — I just can't stop using it
item.description: I haven't stopped using SongDNA since Spotify released it — this is how my first hands-on with the feature went.

item.title: 'It's going to get darker': The Gentlemen season 2 star confirms new episodes are 'finished' — but even more unhinged
item.description: The Gentlemen season 2 will return to Netflix later this year — and its lead star has confirmed that the new episodes are even 'darker'.

item.title: This turbulence-tracking travel app will make your next trip more tolerable
item.description: Turbli tells you exactly what to expect for any specific flight at every moment along the way—from takeoff through landing. When we talk about travel apps, we typically talk about the types of tools that help you organize your itineraries, find worthwhile stops along your way, or maybe even just find flights (and/or fuel!) in the first place.

item.title: Managing AI has become its own job
item.description: AI was supposed to save time. Instead, many workers are bogged down with prompting, checking, and fixing flawed output. Managers are rushing to deploy AI for efficiency gains. Employees have to figure out how to make it work—and that’s sometimes harder than it seems.

item.title: Why Gen Z is fangirling over Apple’s ‘Finder Guy’ mascot
item.description: Everyone loves Apple’s new TikTok mascot—and for good reason. At first, he appeared in the top corner of a multi-slide TikTok post. Then he was spotted demurely relaxing in a lawn chair on a livestream. Finally, on March 30, Apple’s new mascot, nicknamed “Finder Guy,” made his debut—and the internet has instantly become enamored with him.

item.title: J’ai testé la Xiaomi Watch 5, une montre Wear OS plutôt accessible et à l’autonomie impressionnante
item.description: Avec sa Xiaomi Watch 5, le constructeur chinois propose une montre connectée sous Wear OS, mais avec une belle promesse d'autonomie en plus des nombreuses applications compatibles. Voici son test complet.
